mission.title: atlantis: Atlantis outputs all refreshed resources before outputting the final plan output in the PR comments (tf 0.14.2)
mission.description: Atlantis outputs all refreshed resources before outputting the final plan output in the PR comments (tf 0.14.2). This issue affects 23+ users.

steps.title: Identify atlantis troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your atlantis deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis -n atlantis --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review atlantis configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant atlantis configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
kubectl get configmap -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=atlantis
```
What the title says.

I am using atlantis v0.15.1 and tf v0.14.2 and getting this:

while the plan output should only include:

```
No changes. Infrastructure is up-to-date.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Atlantis outputs all refreshed resources before outputting…
steps.description:
Attempt at fixing #1306 

Might not be the right place for this logic; first time committer, please let me know if there is a better place to do this

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Atlantis outputs all refreshed resources before… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis -n atlantis --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n atlantis --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: A community PR (#1320) was opened to fix this issue but was closed without being merged. No merged fix currently exists upstream. This mission is proposed as a guide for users to work around the issue manually until an official fix is merged.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with atlantis installed or the issue environment reproducible.
